title: Parallax Operation Algebra (m-op-algebra)
description: Canonical, format-agnostic serialization of an m-op-algebra operation node. Each node is a single-key tagged object. The temporal algebra addresses axes by the closed dimensions `validTime` and `transactionTime`: `asOf` carries `dimension` plus `coordinate`, `asOfRange` carries `dimension` plus finite `start`/`end`, and `history` carries `dimension`. Omitted dimensions default to Latest; an explicit Latest coordinate is serialized as `latest`, while Now is a finite clock instant serialized as an ordinary timestamp. Physical interval predicates are injected by m-temporal-read and never authored in the operation. The remaining nodes cover the full predicate, relationship, aggregation, nested-value-object, result-directive, and inheritance-narrowing algebra.

attributeRef.description: A metamodel attribute reference of the form Class.attribute.

entityName.description: A bare metamodel entity name (no dotted member) — the `Class` grammar the attribute/relationship refs use before the dot. Used by `narrow` to name a polymorphic entity position and each authored subtype it narrows to (m-op-algebra, m-inheritance).

relationshipRef.description: A metamodel relationship reference of the form Class.relationship.

aggregateAlias.description: An aggregate result-column name (the `as` of an aggregate function). Lowercase, snake_case allowed (e.g. total_quantity, sample_count), matching the canonical SQL alias.

pathSegment.description: One hop in a deep-fetch navigation path: a CLOSED object naming the relationship to traverse via `rel` (a `Class.relationship` reference) and OPTIONALLY narrowing that hop's polymorphic target with `narrow`. The object shape is the single structural carrier for a hop, so a POLYMORPHIC hop (a relationship whose target is an abstract root / abstract subtype, m-inheritance) may carry `narrow` alongside `rel` without a second spelling of a path. A broad hop (no `narrow`) populates the ORDINARY relationship view keyed by the local relationship name; a narrowed hop populates a DISTINCT narrowed view keyed `<rel>[<Concrete>,<Concrete>]` (the effective concrete set, canonical alphabetical order by entity name, no spaces — m-deep-fetch), and does NOT mark the broad relationship loaded. Two authored spellings resolving to the same effective set (`narrow.to: [Pet]` vs `[Cat, Dog]`) derive the SAME view key and DEDUPLICATE to one hop; a broad hop and a narrowed hop over the same relationship are DISTINCT hops.

pathNarrow.description: The subtype narrowing of ONE deep-fetch hop: constrain the relationship's polymorphic target to a NON-EMPTY subset of its subtypes (m-inheritance). Unlike the operation-position `narrow` node (which carries `entity` + `operand`), a path narrow carries ONLY `to` — the position is the relationship's target (implicit) and there is no inner predicate, because a hop eagerly fetches a whole VIEW rather than filtering. Each `to` entry is an authored subtype name (an abstract subtype resolves to its concrete descendants, a concrete subtype to itself; the serde keeps the authored spelling). Validation resolves `to` to the effective concrete set and accepts iff it is NON-EMPTY and a SUBSET of the relationship target's effective concrete set (`narrow-outside-relationship-target` otherwise, m-navigate / m-deep-fetch).

navigationPath.description: An ordered list of path SEGMENTS naming one path to eager-fetch, e.g. [{ rel: 'Order.items' }] (one hop) or [{ rel: 'Order.items' }, { rel: 'OrderItem.product' }] (multi-hop). Each segment is a closed object (`pathSegment`) rather than a bare string, so broad and narrowed hops share one uniform shape; a polymorphic hop MAY carry `narrow` (subtype narrowing of that hop's target).

literal.description: A scalar literal value usable as a bind.

stringMatch.description: A string predicate. `value` is a plain string; `like`/`notLike` interpret `%`/`_` as wildcards, the affix forms (startsWith/endsWith/contains) treat `value` as a literal and the implementation escapes wildcards. `caseInsensitive` defaults to false.

group.description: An explicit precedence-nesting node. It wraps a single inner operation so that a fluent or prefix surface that intends to group a sub-expression serializes to one unambiguous canonical form.

orderBy.description: A result-shaping directive: order an inner query's rows by one or more keys.

limit.description: A result-shaping directive: cap an inner query's row count.

distinct.description: A result-shaping directive: deduplicate an inner query's rows.

navigationFilter.description: Filter the queried entity by traversing a relationship. `rel` names the relationship; the optional `op` constrains the related entity. With no `op` it is a pure existence check.

navigate.description: navigate(rel) -> predicate: filter via traversal to a related entity. Lowers to a correlated EXISTS semi-join, so a to-many navigation never multiplies parent rows.

exists.description: exists(rel[, op]): the queried entity has at least one related row (optionally matching `op`). Lowers to a correlated EXISTS.

notExists.description: notExists(rel[, op]): the queried entity has no related row (optionally matching `op`). Lowers to a correlated NOT EXISTS.

deepFetch.description: An eager-fetch directive: resolve the inner query, then fetch each named navigation path with ONE additional statement per relationship level (N+1 elimination). The result is an object graph, not a flat row set.

narrow.description: narrow(entity, to, operand): constrain a POLYMORPHIC entity position to a subset of its subtypes (m-op-algebra, m-inheritance). `entity` names the polymorphic position in whose operand scope this node appears — the queried entity at top level (the read's `targetEntity`), or the RELATIONSHIP TARGET inside a navigation filter's `op` (`exists` / `navigate` / `notExists`), where the active position is the related entity the hop reaches (m-navigate, resolved Q10). `to` is the non-empty, ORDER-PRESERVED list of authored subtype names the position is narrowed to (an abstract subtype resolves to its concrete descendants, a concrete subtype to itself; the serde keeps the authored spelling). `operand` is the inner operation evaluated over the narrowed position, so a concrete-subtype-declared attribute becomes referenceable there. Semantic validation (never the serde) threads the ACTIVE polymorphic position as it descends — the read's `targetEntity` at top level (defaulting to the family root), the enclosing narrow's resolved set inside a nested narrow. It resolves `entity` and CLAMPS it to (intersects it with) the active position — an `entity` naming a broader position is narrowed to the active position, never allowed to re-widen it — then resolves each `to` entry to its effective concrete-subtype set and accepts iff their union is NON-EMPTY and a SUBSET of that clamped/active position; the resolved set becomes the active position for `operand`, so a nested narrow cannot broaden back out. Redundant narrowing (to the position itself) is valid; broadening beyond the active position is not (`narrow-outside-position`); a `to` resolving to the empty set is `narrow-empty-effective-set` (m-inheritance / m-op-algebra). SQL lowering lifts the narrow into tag/branch selection (m-sql).

aggregateFunction.description: An aggregate function applied to an attribute (m-agg sub-area). `as` is the result column name. `count` MAY omit `attr` to count whole rows in the group (count(*)); every other function requires `attr`.

aggregateBody.description: An aggregate over a named attribute, aliased by `as`.

countBody.description: count(attr) (non-NULL values) or count(*) when `attr` is omitted.

havingComparison.description: An aggregate comparison: an aggregate function compared against a literal. The leaf of a having expression.

havingExpression.description: A boolean expression over aggregate comparisons (having leaves) composed with and/or. A having leaf is one of eq/notEq/gt/gte/lt/lte over an aggregate function.

groupBy.description: The aggregation node (m-agg sub-area). Groups `operand`'s rows by `keys` (omit for a whole-table aggregate), projects one or more aggregate functions, and optionally filters groups with `having`. Lowers to a GROUP BY / HAVING SELECT via m-sql-agg.

temporalCoordinate.description: A temporal point: the literal `latest` or a finite ISO-8601 UTC instant. A finite instant obtained from the current clock is Now; `now` is not a wire alias.

finiteTemporalInstant.description: A finite ISO-8601 UTC instant; `latest` and `now` are not valid range bounds.

asOf.description: asOf(dimension, coordinate): pin one temporal dimension to Latest or a finite instant. Latest lowers to end = infinity; a finite current-clock instant is Now and lowers to interval containment. An omitted declared dimension defaults to Latest. A Bitemporal operation may nest one wrapper per dimension in Valid-Time-then-Transaction-Time order.

asOfRange.description: asOfRange(dimension, start, end): scan a temporal dimension across a finite half-open instant range, returning every milestone that overlaps [start, end).

history.description: history(dimension): return the full milestone set for a temporal dimension. No as-of predicate is injected on that dimension.

nestedRef.description: A reference into a valueObject's inner field: `Class.valueObject` followed by one or more dotted path segments into the embedded document (e.g. Customer.address.city, Customer.address.geo.lat). Lowers to a dialect-specific text extraction on the value object's backing column (m-sql/m-dialect).

nestedComparison.description: A nested-field comparison: a valueObject inner attribute (referenced by a dotted path) compared against a TYPED literal. The `path` resolves against the model's declared valueObject structure (first segment a declared valueObject on the entity, intermediates nested valueObjects, leaf a declared attribute); the `value` is a polymorphic `literal` whose type MUST match the leaf attribute's declared type (m-op-algebra). The dialect-specific document extraction (m-sql/m-dialect) casts to that declared type before comparing.

nestedMembership.description: A nested-field membership test: a valueObject inner attribute (referenced by a dotted path) tested against a non-empty list of TYPED literals. The `path` resolves against the model's declared valueObject structure (as for `nestedComparison`); each list value is a `literal` whose type MUST match the leaf attribute's declared type (m-op-algebra). Lowers to `<extraction> in (?, ?, …)` with the JSON path bind(s) first, then one bind per list value in order (m-sql/m-dialect).

nestedNullCheck.description: A nested-field presence test: a valueObject inner attribute referenced by a dotted path, with NO compared value. The `path` resolves against the model's declared valueObject structure (as for `nestedComparison`). Per the absence-collapse rule (m-op-algebra) every 'not present' state — SQL NULL column, missing key, JSON `null`, non-object intermediate segment — yields SQL NULL from the extraction, so `nestedIsNull` matches exactly those rows and `nestedIsNotNull` its complement.

nestedEq.description: nestedEq(path, value): filter where an embedded valueObject attribute equals a typed literal.

nestedNotEq.description: nestedNotEq(path, value): filter where an embedded valueObject attribute differs from a typed literal.

nestedGt.description: nestedGt(path, value): filter where an embedded valueObject attribute is greater than a typed literal (the extraction is cast to the declared type before comparing).

nestedGte.description: nestedGte(path, value): filter where an embedded valueObject attribute is greater than or equal to a typed literal.

nestedLt.description: nestedLt(path, value): filter where an embedded valueObject attribute is less than a typed literal.

nestedLte.description: nestedLte(path, value): filter where an embedded valueObject attribute is less than or equal to a typed literal.

nestedIn.description: nestedIn(path, values): filter where an embedded valueObject attribute is one of a non-empty list of typed literals.

nestedIsNull.description: nestedIsNull(path): filter where an embedded valueObject attribute is 'not present' (SQL NULL column, missing key, JSON null, or non-object intermediate — all collapsed per m-op-algebra).

nestedIsNotNull.description: nestedIsNotNull(path): filter where an embedded valueObject attribute is present (the complement of nestedIsNull).

valueObjectRef.description: A path that TERMINATES at a valueObject member (not an inner attribute): `Class.valueObject` optionally followed by nested valueObject segments (e.g. Customer.address, Customer.address.phones). Unlike `nestedRef` — which requires an ATTRIBUTE leaf and so has ≥3 components — this path may be just `Class.valueObject` (2 components) because its subject is the value OBJECT itself. It resolves against the declared structure (first segment a declared valueObject on the entity, each further segment a nested valueObject declared on the preceding member); the final member's multiplicity (`one` / `many`) selects the presence vs non-empty semantics of nestedExists/nestedNotExists (m-op-algebra).

elementRef.description: An ELEMENT-RELATIVE path used ONLY inside a scoped `where` (nestedExists/nestedNotExists over a `many` member). It has NO leading `Class.valueObject` — the subject is the array element bound by the enclosing exists — so it is one or more lowercase dotted segments naming declared members of that element (`type`, `number`, or `geo.country` to descend a nested value object of the element). Each segment resolves against the element's declared structure (intermediates nested valueObjects, leaf a declared attribute).

elementComparison.description: An element-scoped nested comparison: an element-relative `path` compared against a TYPED literal whose type MUST match the leaf attribute's declared type. The subject is the array element bound by the enclosing nestedExists/nestedNotExists `where`.

elementMembership.description: An element-scoped membership test: an element-relative `path` tested against a non-empty list of typed literals.

elementNullCheck.description: An element-scoped presence test: an element-relative `path` with NO compared value.

elementPredicate.description: A scoped sub-predicate evaluated against ONE array element inside a nestedExists/nestedNotExists `where`. It is the flat `nested*` family re-expressed over element-relative paths (`elementRef`), composed with the ordinary boolean combinators. The whole compound must be satisfied by a SINGLE element (same-element semantics), which is what distinguishes it from ANDing flat any-element predicates at the top level (m-op-algebra).

nestedExistsBody.description: The body of nestedExists/nestedNotExists: a value-object-terminated `path` and an OPTIONAL element-scoped `where`. Without `where` the meaning is presence — a `one` member present (non-absent per the absence-collapse rule), a `many` member a NON-EMPTY array. With `where` (a `many` path), ONE element must satisfy the whole compound `where` sub-predicate (same-element semantics); nestedNotExists + where means NO element satisfies it (m-op-algebra). Lowers to the m-dialect array-traversal form (m-sql).

nestedExists.description: nestedExists(path[, where]): the value object at `path` is present (`one`) or its array is non-empty (`many`); with `where`, at least one element satisfies the compound sub-predicate.

nestedNotExists.description: nestedNotExists(path[, where]): the complement of nestedExists — the value object is absent (`one`) or the array is empty/absent (`many`); with `where`, no element satisfies the compound sub-predicate.
